title: Postman の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article provides accurate and well-supported technical information regarding Postman 401 errors, their causes, and solutions.
Claims are generally consistent with official Postman documentation and general API authentication standards.
The advice on securely handling API keys and tokens in Postman is aligned with current best practices.

sources.title: 401 Unauthorized - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: HTTP 401 Unauthorized indicates that the request lacks valid authentication credentials.

sources.title: 401エラー（Unauthorized）とは？原因から解決方法・予防策まで初心者にもわかりやすく解説
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized error occurs when authentication information is insufficient or invalid.

sources.title: 401エラー（HTTP 401 Unauthorized）とは - IT用語辞典 e-Words
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized means access permission confirmation is required for the requested resource, returned on initial access to protected resources or failed authentication.

sources.title: HTTP 401 ステータスコード (Unauthorized) - Amazon CloudFront
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized status code means a client's request is not completed due to lack of valid authentication information.

sources.title: 401エラー(Unauthorized)とは？概要や発生原因、対処方法、混同しやすいHTTPレスポンスステータスコードなども解説！
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized is an HTTP response status code displayed when authentication fails or access rights are missing for a website.

sources.title: The Postman Data Leak - Exposing the Hidden Risks of API Development - Vorlon
sources.claim: Publicly accessible Postman collections can expose sensitive data like API keys, access tokens, and credentials.

sources.title: HTTP 401 Unauthorized Error: Comprehensive Guide, Causes, and Fixes - SurferCloud
sources.claim: HTTP 401 status code is returned when a client tries to access a resource needing authentication but fails to provide valid credentials, or omits them.

sources.title: Lessons from the Postman Data Breach: API Security Gaps & Solutions - CybelAngel
sources.claim: Misconfigured Postman workspaces can lead to sensitive credentials like usernames, passwords, API keys, and tokens being leaked.

sources.title: RFC 7235 - Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1): Authentication 日本語訳
sources.claim: The 401 (Unauthorized) status code indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource. The server generating a 401 response MUST send a WWW-Authenticate header field.

sources.title: 30,000 Postman workspaces leak API keys & credentials - SecurityBrief Asia
sources.claim: More than 30,000 publicly accessible Postman workspaces exposed sensitive information such as access tokens, refresh tokens, and third-party API keys.

sources.title: Stop Exposing Secrets! Secure Your APIs in Postman Like a Pro - Medium
sources.claim: Postman's environment variables are managed in two stages: Initial Values (synced to Postman cloud) and Current Values (local to your machine). Sensitive data should be stored as Current Value.

sources.title: How Secure Are Your APIs in Postman? - Help Hub
sources.claim: Postman environment variables handle Initial Value (shared with others) and Current Value (stays on your device only). For sensitive data, leave Initial Value blank and use Current Value.

sources.title: 'Error 401 – Unauthorized (Authentication Failed: JWT token is not valid)' when executing REST APIs - Revenera Community
sources.claim: Expired authentication token: JWT tokens have a validity period. If the token has expired, authentication will fail.

sources.title: API Testing(Part-8). Variables & Environment in Postman | by Amrut Raibagi | Medium
sources.claim: Postman variables increase the user's efficiency to work and decrease the errors, allowing values to be stored and used throughout requests, environments, collections, and scripts.

sources.title: API Security Best Practices: A Developer's Guide to Protecting Your APIs - Postman Blog
sources.claim: It is recommended to store secrets in environment variables or a dedicated secrets manager. Expired or malformed tokens will result in a 401 Unauthorized response.

sources.title: Securely using API keys in Postman
sources.claim: Store API keys in variables, use initial and current values, and do not share API keys with your team by only updating the current value.

sources.title: Postman Data Leaks: The Hidden Risks Lurking in Your Workspaces | CloudSEK
sources.claim: Sensitive data leaks in Postman workspaces pose significant risks, exposing API keys, credentials, and tokens.

sources.title: 401 Unauthorized | httpstatus.io
sources.claim: The 401 Unauthorized status code indicates the client attempting to access a resource has not provided valid authentication credentials for that resource, or they are incorrect or expired.

sources.title: Mastering Postman: Environment and Global Variables - EchoAPI
sources.claim: Postman recommends managing API keys and tokens as environment variables for streamlined API testing, reduced errors, and increased efficiency.

sources.title: What is JWT expiration? - Inventive HQ
sources.claim: JWT expiration limits the lifetime of tokens, automatically restricting access if a token is compromised. The 'exp' (expiration time) claim specifies when a token becomes invalid.

sources.title: Security & Shared Responsibility - Postman
sources.claim: Be careful when publishing a Postman element to avoid accidental data exposure by making it public or sharing too broadly with team members.

sources.title: Authorization types supported by Postman
sources.claim: Postman's official support site (https://learning.postman.com/docs/sending-requests/authorization/) contains detailed setup guides for each authentication type. Postman supports various authorization types like 'No Auth', 'API Key', 'Bearer Token'.

sources.title: How to Use Variables in Postman (Global, Environment, Collection, Local) - YouTube
sources.claim: Postman variables help avoid hard-coding values, switch between environments, build dynamic workflows, and collaborate effectively. It is recommended to manage API keys and tokens as environment variables.

sources.title: Fixing a 401 Unauthorized response error - Postman Docs
sources.claim: A 401 Unauthorized Error is an HTTP status code that indicates the server received an unauthenticated request due to missing or invalid credentials for the requested resource.

sources.title: HTTP 401 Unauthorized: Causes & Fixes - Authgear
sources.claim: A 401 Unauthorized error means the request reached the server but was rejected because it lacked valid authentication credentials. Common causes include expired JWTs, missing or malformed Authorization headers, revoked API keys, and signing key mismatches.

sources.title: Difference between global and local variables in Postman - GeeksforGeeks
sources.claim: Postman environment variables are managed in two stages: 'Initial Value' (synced to the Postman cloud) and 'Current Value' (local to your machine, not shared).

sources.title: 401 Unauthorized - HTTP
sources.claim: The HTTP 401 Unauthorized client error status response code indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

sources.title: Stop Exposing Secrets! Secure Your APIs in Postman Like a Pro - DZone
sources.claim: Sensitive data should be stored as Current Value, not Initial Value, to prevent accidental exposure when sharing collections.

sources.title: Introducing the Secret Variable Type in Postman
sources.claim: The secret variable type enables masking of initial and current values, preventing secrets from being visible during screen shares or demos.

sources.title: What If We Get 401 Response: Handling Access Token Expiration and Refresh in Android using Retrofit | by Oguz Evirgen | Medium
sources.claim: Access tokens have expiration times, and after this period, the token becomes invalid, leading to a 401 Unauthorized error.

sources.title: Oktaが発行するJSON Webトークン（JWT）の有効期間の概要
sources.claim: When using Org Authorization Server, JSON Web Token (JWT) lifetimes are hardcoded (e.g., ID tokens: 60 minutes, Access tokens: 60 minutes, Refresh tokens: 90 days).

sources.title: Explain the use of environment variables in Postman requests. - GeeksforGeeks
sources.claim: Postman recommends managing API keys or tokens as environment variables for security and reusability.

sources.title: Reuse data with variables and environments in Postman | Postman Docs
sources.claim: Variables enable you to reuse data throughout your requests and change values based on your working environment.

sources.title: 【セキュリティ】JWT の有効期限（exp）は変更できるのか？ - Qiita
sources.claim: JWT (JSON Web Token) typically has an expiration date ('exp') set for security reasons. Expired JWT tokens will be deemed invalid.

sources.title: 【セキュリティ】JWT Lifetimes #Security - Qiita
sources.claim: JWT has an expiration date ('exp') as an important check element. Without proper 'exp' management, an attacker could use a compromised token indefinitely.

sources.title: Correct HTTP code for authentication token expiry - 401 or 403? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: An expired token means that the token was successfully parsed but that the expiration date set in that token is already passed, which typically results in a 401.

sources.title: Postman best practices: How Raygun's engineering team uses Postman to improve our API workflow · Raygun Blog
sources.claim: An environment in Postman is a set of key-value variables which can be used to manage values that differ across environments.

sources.title: JWTの有効期限とリフレッシュトークンの管理戦略 - ISSUE
sources.claim: JWT (JSON Web Token) typically has an 'exp' (expiration time) claim set, usually ranging from minutes to hours, with shorter durations recommended for security.

sources.title: Postman Client gets 401 where Postman Chrome Extension does not
sources.claim: Missing headers in the Postman app can cause a 401 error.

sources.title: Postman CLI_Collection Run_401 Unauthorized_ERROR - Help Hub
sources.claim: Postman CLI failing with '401 Unauthorized' can occur if the environment variables for authentication are not correctly picked up from the local file.

sources.title: Authenticate with OAuth 2.0 authentication in Postman | Postman Docs
sources.claim: OAuth 2.0 grant types often involve exchanging an authorization code for an access token to authenticate later requests.

sources.title: 401エラー(Unauthorized)の原因と解決策！JWT認証からSEO対策まで - IRORI by Cominka
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized indicates that the server has rejected access because it cannot verify who you are, often due to incorrect ID/password, expired login sessions/tokens, or authentication setting misconfigurations.

sources.title: Post manのレスポンスで401が返ってきた。 - Qiita
sources.claim: A 401 Unauthorized response indicates a request made without proper authentication information, often due to missing a bearer token in the headers.

sources.title: Issue: Authorization Works in Postman App, but 401 in Postman CLI - Help Hub
sources.claim: When environment variables are not correctly referenced, or a different environment is selected in Postman CLI, it can lead to 401 Unauthorized errors because the token might be referenced dynamically in the Authorization header.

sources.title: Add API authorization details to requests in Postman | Postman Docs
sources.claim: When selecting an auth type from the Authorization tab, Postman indicates which parts of the request your details are included in. It is recommended to use your Postman Vault to store sensitive data.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Ensure all code examples are directly verifiable against Postman's current UI/features for maximum accuracy.
Optional improvement: Periodically review links to external articles/blogs to ensure they remain active and relevant.
Optional improvement: Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
